title: CAMS-integrated drone delivery corridors in Brussels
summary: A drone delivery operator integrated CAMS air-quality forecasts into route planning to keep BVLOS corridors out of high-pollution zones.
narrative:
## Challenge
Planning beyond-visual-line-of-sight delivery corridors in a dense city, the operator needed to avoid concentrating flights in high-pollution areas, both for regulatory acceptance and to limit particulate wear on UAV systems.

## Approach
CAMS European air-quality forecasts at high resolution, updated four times daily, were integrated into the operator's route-planning API. Corridors exceeding PM2.5 and NO2 thresholds were flagged automatically and alternative paths generated.

## Results
Over a six-month pilot, around 12% of planned sorties were rerouted. Regulators accepted the CAMS-integrated approach as part of the operational authorisation, setting a precedent for CAMS use in U-Space applications.
